Title: The Effector RipAW Enhances Ralstonia solanacearum Invasion in Arabidopsis via CBP60g/SARD1-Dependent and -Independent Pathways

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III secreted effector; E3 ubiquitin ligase
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immune signaling and resistance to pathogen infection
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances R. solanacearum proliferation and plant susceptibility; suppresses SA-mediated immunity without increasing SA accumulation; also alters root architecture via a CBP60g/SARD1-independent pathway
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: inducible transgenic expression; deletion mutant and complementation; point mutation abolishing E3 ligase activity; bacterial growth assays; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; protein stability assay; split-luciferase; BiFC; co-immunoprecipitation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: β-estradiol induction; Ralstonia solanacearum infection; CHX treatment; MG132 treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transgene induced in roots by β-estradiol
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RipAW is required for full virulence of R. solanacearum in Arabidopsis. 2. RipAW E3 ligase activity is essential for promoting bacterial growth and inducing root architecture changes. 3. RipAW associates with and destabilizes CBP60g in a proteasome- and E3 ligase-dependent manner. 4. RipAW-mediated promotion of bacterial proliferation depends genetically on CBP60g/SARD1, whereas RipAW-induced root architecture changes do not.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering bacterial wilt resistance by disabling RipAW recognition targets or blocking its E3 ligase activity/host interaction surfaces
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Constitutive expression causes severe dwarfism and sterility in Arabidopsis
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: RipAW upregulates CBP60g and SARD1 transcripts but does not activate ICS1, PR1, or SA accumulation, implying post-transcriptional suppression of immunity through CBP60g destabilization; also reported to target FLS2-XLG2-BIK1 complex in discussion/background.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immune signaling and disease resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Expression was not altered by RipAW and did not contribute as an experimentally manipulated core target in this study
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Roots; unchanged in Est::RipAW plants under β-estradiol treatment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 transcript levels were measured to test whether RipAW-induced CBP60g/SARD1 upregulation activates SA biosynthesis. 2. ICS1 expression remained unchanged in Est::RipAW roots. 3. Together with unchanged SA levels, this indicates SA biosynthesis was not activated.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because directly assayed in Results as SA biosynthesis marker, but not a primary manipulated gene controlling phenotype in this paper.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immune signaling and resistance to bacterial infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of SA biosynthetic genes; negative post-translational regulation by RipAW-mediated destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Normally activates ICS1; in Est::RipAW plants CBP60g transcript is induced but downstream ICS1/PR1 activation is not observed due to protein destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a positive regulator of resistance to R. solanacearum; loss of function increases bacterial growth and disease, while RipAW associates with and destabilizes CBP60g to promote pathogen proliferation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; cbp60g/sard1 mutant infection assays; transient co-expression protein stability assay with CHX/MG132; split-luciferase; BiFC; co-immunoprecipitation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: β-estradiol induction; R. solanacearum infection; CHX treatment; MG132 treatment
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcripts strongly upregulated in Est::RipAW lines after β-estradiol treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g transcript is strongly induced in RipAW-expressing Arabidopsis roots. 2. CBP60g protein associates with RipAW and is destabilized in an E3 ligase- and proteasome-dependent manner. 3. cbp60g/sard1 mutants are more susceptible to R. solanacearum. 4. Loss of CBP60g/SARD1 restores growth of the ΔripAW mutant, indicating RipAW virulence depends on suppressing this immune node.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive immune regulator required for resistance-associated SA pathway competence, but rendered ineffective by RipAW-induced protein destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible compensatory transcriptional feedback inferred in discussion because transcript increases despite protein destabilization
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering CBP60g variants resistant to RipAW-mediated destabilization may improve bacterial wilt resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although transcript increased, SA marker genes and SA content remained unchanged, indicating uncoupling between transcript abundance and functional output.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Systemic Acquired Resistance Deficient 1; CBP60 family-related immune regulator
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: plant immune signaling and resistance to bacterial infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation of SA biosynthetic genes; proposed negative regulation by RipAW during infection
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Normally activates ICS1 together with CBP60g; however ICS1/PR1 were not induced in Est::RipAW plants despite SARD1 transcript upregulation
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of resistance to R. solanacearum; loss of function with cbp60g increases susceptibility and genetically restores ΔripAW bacterial growth defect
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; cbp60g/sard1 mutant infection assays; genetic analysis with ΔripAW and Est::RipAW lines
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root transcripts strongly upregulated in Est::RipAW lines after β-estradiol treatment
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 transcript is strongly induced in RipAW-expressing roots. 2. cbp60g/sard1 mutant plants show increased bacterial growth, accelerated wilting and lower survival. 3. Loss of CBP60g/SARD1 restores proliferation of ΔripAW, showing RipAW virulence depends on this immune module. 4. CBP60g/SARD1 are not required for RipAW-mediated root architecture changes.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive immune regulator acting redundantly with CBP60g to support SA-related defense output against R. solanacearum
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Possible compensatory feedback on transcript abundance discussed but not experimentally proven
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing SARD1 function may strengthen bacterial wilt resistance, especially together with CBP60g protection
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct biochemical interaction with RipAW was not tested because SARD1 expression in N. benthamiana was too low to detect.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional response marker downstream of SA
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PR1 expression was unchanged in Est::RipAW plants, supporting the conclusion that SA signaling was not activated
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR1 was assayed as an SA pathway marker. 2. Its expression was not induced by RipAW. 3. This supports suppression/inactivity of SA signaling despite CBP60g/SARD1 transcript induction.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Not a decisive regulator; used only as downstream marker
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a core gene by strict criterion, but directly measured in Results as a readout of SA signaling.
